question: List the five most recent questions or debates raised in the Lok Sabha regarding the establishment of committees for preventing aviation accidents and the growth of electric vehicles.
answer:
I cannot directly answer this specific question based on the provided parliamentary records, as the available context is a limited selection of documents rather than a complete chronological list of all Lok Sabha proceedings. However, based on the records provided, the following are the five most recent questions or reports related to the establishment of committees for preventing aviation accidents and the growth/safety of electric vehicles, presented in reverse chronological order:

*   **31st July 2025: Measures to Prevent Air Accidents (Unstarred Question No. 1994)**
    *   This inquiry addresses **08 air accidents** reported in **2025** (as of July), involving scheduled aircraft, trainee aircraft, and helicopters [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Measures to Prevent Air Accidents].
    *   The **Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB)** is identified as the body investigating these incidents under the **Aircraft (Investigation of Accidents & Incidents) Rules, 2017** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Measures to Prevent Air Accidents].
    *   The response notes that India complies with **International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO)** standards through Civil Aviation Requirements (CARs) issued by the **Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Measures to Prevent Air Accidents].

*   **24th July 2025: Establishment of Committee for Preventing Aviation Accidents (Unstarred Question No. 739)**
    *   Following the crash of **Air India Flight AI-171** in Ahmedabad on **12.06.2025**, a **High-Level Committee** was constituted on **13.06.2025** under the chairmanship of the **Union Home Secretary** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Establishment of Committee for preventing Aviation Accidents].
    *   The committee is mandated to submit its report within **three months** and focuses on formulating **Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)** for accident prevention and handling [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Establishment of Committee for preventing Aviation Accidents].
    *   It is explicitly stated that this committee will not substitute other ongoing inquiries by relevant organizations [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Establishment of Committee for preventing Aviation Accidents].

*   **Circa 2022/2023: Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles (Unstarred Question No. 1534)**
    *   This question addresses fire incidents in electric two-wheelers from manufacturers such as **Okinawa Autotech, Pure EV, and Ola Electric** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles].
    *   An investigating team of experts from **DRDO**, the **Indian Institute of Science (IISC) Bengaluru**, and the **Naval Science & Technological Laboratory (NSTL)** was constituted to identify root causes [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles].
    *   Based on committee recommendations, the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways amended the **Automotive Industry Standards (AIS:156 and AIS:038)** on **28th September 2022**, with specific clauses becoming effective on **31st March 2023** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles].

*   **18th July 2018: Aircraft Crashed (Unstarred Question No. 108)**
    *   This record focuses on accidents involving **Defence aircraft** (Army, Air Force, and Navy) between the financial years **2015-16** and **2018-19 (up to 09.07.2018)** [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Aircraft Crashed].
    *   The Ministry stated that every accident is investigated by a **Court of Inquiry** to ascertain causes and implement recommendations for non-recurrence [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Aircraft Crashed].

*   **Post-April 2018: Policy on Electric Vehicle Sectors (Unstarred Question No. 400)**
    *   The government identified **NITI Aayog** as the nodal agency for promoting electric vehicles and clean mobility solutions [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Policy on Electric Vehicle Sectors].
    *   It clarifies that under the **Electricity Act, 2003**, the charging of electric vehicle batteries through charging stations does not require a license, allowing any entity to set up stations [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Policy on Electric Vehicle Sectors].

### **Evolution of Policy and Oversight**
*   **Aviation Safety:** Between **2017-2018** and **2025**, the focus evolved from general **Courts of Inquiry** for military aircraft [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Aircraft Crashed] to the establishment of a **civil High-Level Committee** chaired by the Home Secretary to create comprehensive, multi-stakeholder **SOPs** for large-scale civil aviation disasters [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Establishment of Committee for preventing Aviation Accidents].
*   **Electric Vehicles:** Early records (circa **2018**) focused on designating **NITI Aayog** as the lead agency and deregulating charging infrastructure [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Policy on Electric Vehicle Sectors]. By **2022**, the focus shifted toward safety regulations and the technical standardisation of **traction batteries** following fire incidents [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles].

UNSTARRED QUESTION NO. : 739 ( TO BE ED ON THE 24th July 2025 ) ESTABLISHMENT OF COMMITTEE FOR PREVENTING AVIATION ACCIDENTS 739. SHRI EATALA RAJENDER SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY SMT D K ARUNA ADV. ADOOR PRAKASH SHRI SELVAGANAPATHI T.M. Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:- (a) whether the Government has set up a high-level committee/task force to probe the crash of Air India plane AI-171 in Ahmedabad and to suggest “comprehensive guidelines” to respond to aviation accidents against the backdrop of the said crash; (b) if so, the details thereof along with the present status of the said committee and the action taken on its reports till date; (c) if not, the reasons therefor and the time by which such a committee would be set up to prepare a roadmap to prevent accidents and ensure compensation to the victim families; (d) whether it is a fact that the said committee would focus on formulating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for preventing and handling such occurrences in the future and publish its report within three months and if so, the details thereof; (e) whether it is also true that the proposed committee would not substitute other inquiries being conducted by relevant organizations and if so, the details thereof; and (f) whether there was any lack of rescue operations when the air crash took place on 12.6.2025 in Ahmedabad which prompted the Government to constitute such a panel and if so, the details thereof? Minister of State in the (Shri Murlidhar Mohol) (a) to (f): A High-Level Committee was constituted on 13.06.2025 under the chairmanship of the Union Home Secretary to suggest comprehensive guidelines for dealing with such instances in the future post Air India Flight AI-171 incident. The Committee is to submit its report within three months. The key objectives of the Committee are given below:; 1) Recommend necessary improvements and formulate suitable SOP to prevent such incidents in the future. 2) Suggest policy changes, operational improvements and training enhancements required to prevent such occurrences and handle post-crash incident situation. 3) Assess emergency response of various stakeholders (both Central and State Governments), including rescue operations, and coordination among them. ; High-Level Committee has been constituted to suggest comprehensive guidelines for dealing with such instances in the future. The Committee will not be a substitute to other enquiries being conducted by relevant organisations but will focus on formulating SOPs for preventing and handling such occurrences in the future.

DEPARTMENT OF DEFENCE LOK SABHA UNSTARRED QUESTION NO.892 TO BE ED ON THE 20TH DECEMBER, 2017 DEATH OF PILOTS 892. SHRIMATI KAVITHA KALVAKUNTLA: Will the Minister of DEFENCE j{kk ea=h be pleased to state: (a) the number of pilots who lost their lives on duty during the last three years; (b) the number of helicopters and aircrafts destroyed during the course of official duty in the same period; (c) whether any remedial checks and balances have been instituted by the Government to ensure safety of lives; and (d) if so, the details thereof? A N S W E R MINISTER OF STATE (DR. SUBHASH BHAMRE) IN THE र ा रा य मं ी (डा. सुभाष भामरे) (a) & (b): During the last three years (FY 2014-15 to 2016-17), a total of 35 Defence aircraft, including 11 helicopters have crashed resulting in death of 14 pilots. (c) & (d): Every aircraft accident/incident is thoroughly investigated by a Court /Board of Inquiry to ascertain the cause of accident and the recommendations of the completed Court/Board of Inquiry are implemented. Various preventive measures are being taken including invigoration of Aviation Safety Organization, streamlining of accident/ incident reporting procedure, analytical studies and quality audits of the aircraft fleets to identify vulnerable areas to avoid aircraft accidents. Accident prevention programmes have been given an added thrust to identify risk prone/hazardous areas specific to the aircraft fleets and operational environment to ensure safe practices/procedures.

UNSTARRED QUESTION NO.400 TO BE POLICY ON ELECTRIC VEHICLE SECTORS 400. SHRI OM BIRLA: Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state: (a) whether the Government has formulated any consolidated policy on electric vehicle sector development in the country; (b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; (c) whether the Government has taken any initiative to encourage the growth of electric charging stations across various States for encouraging the growth of electric vehicle sale; (d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and (e) whether the Government has accepted the report of the NITI Aayog and Rocky Mountain Institute regarding the electric vehicles which recommended the establishment of commercial manufacturing consortiums for batteries, common components and platforms in the country to prepare it for the future, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor? A N S W E R THE MINISTER OF STATE (INDEPENDENT CHARGE) FOR POWER AND NEW & RENEWABLE ENERGY ( SHRI R. K. SINGH ) (a) & (b) : NITI Aayoghas been designated as the nodal agency for promotion of Electric Vehicles/ clean mobility solutions. Information is being collected. (c) & (d) : Regarding the setting up of Charging Stations for Electric Vehicles, .4.2018 that the charging of batteries of electric vehicles through charging stations does not require any license under the provisions of Electricity Act, 2003. Accordingly, any person/entity is free to set up Charging Stations in the country. (e) : NITI Aayog has been designated as the nodal agency for promotion of Electric Vehicles/ clean mobility solutions. Information is being collected.

[SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Measures to Prevent Air Accidents]
UNSTARRED QUESTION NO. : 1994 ( TO BE ED ON THE 31st July 2025 ) MEASURES TO PREVENT AIR ACCIDENTS 1994. SHRI BAJRANG MANOHAR SONWANE SHRI SANJAY HARIBHAU JADHAV Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:- (a) whether the Government has conducted any investigations into the recent air accidents in the country and if so, the details thereof; (b) whether the causes of these air accidents have been identified and if so, the findings thereof; (c) the total number of fatalities and injuries caused due to air accidents in different parts of the country; (d) whether any compensation or assistance is provided to the families of passengers who lost their lives or were injured in air accidents; (e) if so, the details of compensation amount disbursed during the last three years; (f) whether any specific training programmes is being organised by the Government for pilots, ground staff and other relevant personnel to prevent such accidents in the future and if so, the details thereof; (g) whether all aircraft operating in the country are equipped with modern technologies, if so, the details thereof and if not, the details of the plan to upgrade them technologically; and (h) whether the Government has adopted international norms to prevent air accidents and if so, the details thereof? Minister of State in the (Shri Murlidhar Mohol) (a) to (c): There have been a total of 08 accidents (involving 1 scheduled aircraft, 3 trainee aircraft and 4 helicopters) reported in the year 2025 (till date). The details are enclosed as Annexure A. The cause of these accidents is under investigation by Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) as per the Aircraft (Investigation of Accidents & Incidents) Rules, 2017.; ; (d) & (e): Payment of compensation to the passenger or Next of Kin in case of death or bodily injury to the passenger, caused by air accident / incident is governed by the provisions of Carriage by Air Act, 1972. India has ratified the Montreal Convention, 1999 by making amendments to the Carriage by Air Act, 1972 in 2009. As per the said act, the liability of payment of compensation in case of death, delay, damage or loss to persons, baggage or cargo for international carriage, is of the carriers.; ; (f) to (h): Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has issued Civil Aviation Requirements (CARs) which are at par with international standards. All pilots are required to undergo training as per the training syllabus provided by the OEM (aircraft manufacturer). In addition, training can be undertaken only in DGCA approved Flying Training Organisations (FTOs) / Aviation Training Organisations (ATOs). DGCA ensures FTOs' quality and safety through regular surveillance under its Annual Surveillance Plan (ASP).; ; Ground staff are trained by the operators as per the Ground Handling Manual. The same applies to the other relevant staff. ; ; All aircraft engaged in scheduled commercial operations in the country are equipped with modern technologies.; ; India being a signatory to the Chicago Convention, 1944, complies with all the International standards provided under the annexes of the convention. Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) issues Civil Aviation Requirements (CAR) from time to time, complying with the International Safety Standards and Regulations established by the ICAO to ensure safe and reliable air transport services. [SOURCE: UNKNOWN - Incidents of Fire in Electric Vehicles]
UN INCIDENTS OF FIRE IN ELECTRIC VEHICLES 1534. SHRI HIBI EDEN: Will the Minister of HEAVY INDUSTRIES भारी उद्योग मंत्री be pleased to state: (a) whether the Government has the details and the number of incidents of fire in vehicles due to the defective battery in hybrid electric vehicles, plug-in hybrid electric vehicles and other battery-operated electric vehicles manufactured in the country; (b) if so, the details thereof including the number of deaths occured as a result of the same, State/UTwise and the reasons therefor; (c) whether the Government is taking any measures including adequate testing facilities for imported components for EVs to ensure their assimilation with domestic conditions and if so, the details thereof; (d) whether there is any expert committee to enquire into the above issues; and (e) whether the Government has issued necessary instructions on the defaulting companies, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor? THE MINISTER OF STATE FOR HEAVY INDUSTRIES (SHRI KRISHAN PAL GURJAR) (a) & (b): Yes Sir, as per information received from the Highways, incidents of fire accidents have been reported in Electric two-wheelers, manufactured by Okinawa Autotech, Pure EV, Jitendra Electric Vehicles, Ola Electric and Boom Motors. (c): Yes Sir, the prototypes / components of electric vehicles are tested by the test agencies notified under Rule 126 of Central Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989, for compliance to standards notified by MoRTH. (d): Yes Sir, MoRTH had constituted an investigating team of independent experts from DRDO, Indian Institute of Science (IISC) Bengaluru, and Naval Science & Technological Laboratory (NSTL) Visakhapatnam to investigate into the root cause of fire and recommend remedial measures. experts to suggest formulation of safety standards for the battery and its components, BMS and related systems in electric vehicles. Based on the recommendation of the committee, Ministry of Road Transport and Highways vide S.O. 4567(E) dated 28th September, 2022 has brought amendment to the Automotive Industry Standards, AIS:156 [Specific requirements for L (a motor vehicle less than four wheels and quadricycle) category electric power train vehicles] and PTO -2- AIS:038 (Rev 2) [Specific Requirements for M Category, (a motor vehicle with at least four wheels used for carrying passenger) N Category (a motor vehicle with at least four wheels used for carrying goods) Electric Power Train Vehicles] to prescribe Technical Requirements for Traction Battery of L, M and N Category of Electric Power Train Vehicles. The said Amendments are applicable from 1st December, 2022 and some clauses of these AIS standards will be effective from 31st March, 2023. (e): Yes Sir, MoRTH had issued the draft notification, vide G.S.R 659 (E) dated 25th August 2022, for the requirements of Conformity of Production (COP), in respect of all categories of Electric vehicles including Quadricycles, E- rickshaws, two wheelers, and four wheelers.
